title: audit4me finding frontmatter (v1)
description: YAML frontmatter at the top of each .code4me/audit4me/findings/{id}.md file. Machine-readable metadata; tooling (/audit4me-status, /audit4me-findings, /audit4me-apply) reads this without parsing the markdown body. The body itself is freeform markdown (summary, vendor perspectives, proposed fix, failing test, apply-readiness).

id.description: Finding identifier. Format: F-{YYYY-MM-DD}-{4-digit-sequence}. Sequence is per-day; resets at UTC midnight.

severity.description: Severity level. NIT = style/aesthetic; MINOR = code quality but no functional impact; MAJOR = correctness or security issue that should be fixed; CRITICAL = active or imminent harm (data loss, security breach, availability).

category.description: Audit category that produced this finding.

file.description: Project-relative path of the file the finding is about.

line_range.description: Line range within the file. Single line ('87') or inclusive range ('87-92').

content_hash.description: Content hash of the file at the time the finding was produced. If the file's current hash differs, the finding is stale (apply-readiness check fails).

audited_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the audit pass that produced this finding completed.

vendors_agreed.description: Vendors that independently flagged this finding. Each vendor's verbatim quote appears in the markdown body under '## Evidence'.

vendors_dissented.description: Vendors that audited this file in this category but did not flag this finding. Used to compute dissent in 3-vendor mode.

confidence.description: Composite confidence from multi-vendor agreement, reproducibility (failing test), LSP/linter cross-check, and test-coverage-at-line. high = all signals positive; medium = most positive; low = only agreement.

proposed_fix.description: True if the finding body includes a proposed fix that /audit4me-apply can dispatch. Computed from severity >= propose_fix_min_severity AND failing_test_path populated (if required) AND unanimity (if required).

failing_test_path.description: Project-relative path to a failing test demonstrating the bug (for category=bugs and category=security primarily). Omitted when the auditor couldn't write a test or category doesn't typically warrant one.

rules_version.description: rules_version active when this finding was produced.

status.description: open = awaiting user review or apply. applied = /audit4me-apply has dispatched the fix. dismissed = /audit4me-dismiss marked won't-fix (reason recorded in body). stale = file has changed since the finding was produced; needs re-audit before action.

dismissal_reason.description: Reason recorded when status=dismissed via /audit4me-dismiss. Omitted otherwise.
